titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Arthur Lee Diaries and Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 19, 2021 at 03:38

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Estate of Ingeborg Lee Muller, 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Arthur Lee Diaries and Papers. General Collection. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of this collection consists of diaries kept by sculptor Arthur Lee between 1914 and 1959. It also includes sketchbooks, drawings, photographs, correspondence, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Lee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Arthur Lee Diaries and Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Arthur Lee
bioghist.p: Arthur Lee was an American sculptor and artist. Born in Trondheim, Norway in 1881, he emigrated to the United States with his family at the age of eight. Lee studied at the Arts Students League in New York (where he later taught) and the École des Beaux-Arts in Paris. Lee passed away at his Connecticut home on May 14, 1961.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I: Diaries, 1914 - 1959. II: Artwork, photographs, and other papers, 1924 - 1998. III: Printed materials, 1908 - 1929.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of diaries kept by Arthur Lee between 1914 and 1959.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of sketchbooks and drawings by Arthur Lee, photographs, correspondence, a scrapbook, slides, catalogs and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains photographs of art, notes, printed material and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contains photographs of sculpture and drawings.

did.unittitle: Art Students League of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders contain black and white photographs of drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains black and white photographs of sculptures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of three books owned by Arthur Lee.
